---
title: "PhillyStat360 — Violation Mapping, Baseline & Feature Engineering"
author: "MUSA Practicum Team"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output:
  html_document:
    toc: true
    toc_float: true
    toc_depth: 3
    theme: flatly
    code_folding: show
---

## 0. Setup

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, message = FALSE, warning = FALSE)
```

```{r libraries}
library(tidyverse)
library(janitor)
library(knitr)
library(kableExtra)
library(scales)
library(patchwork)
library(sf)
library(lubridate)
library(broom)
library(pROC)
library(caret)

data_path  <- "../rawdata/"
out_path   <- "../data/"
graph_path <- "../graphs/"

dir.create(out_path,   showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)
dir.create(graph_path, showWarnings = FALSE, recursive = TRUE)

# Temporal Anchor: All predictive variables must strictly precede this date
TRAIN_CUTOFF  <- as.Date("2025-10-01")
RECENT_WINDOW <- TRAIN_CUTOFF - months(6)
```

---

## 1. load data

```{r read-data, cache=TRUE}
properties <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "opa_properties_public.csv"),
                       show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
clean_seal  <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "clean_seal.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
violations  <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "violations.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
licenses    <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "business_licenses.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
unsafe      <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "unsafe.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
imm_dang    <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "imm_dang.csv"),
                        show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()
parcel_sf   <- st_read(paste0(data_path, "PWD_PARCELS.geojson"),
                       quiet = TRUE) %>% clean_names()
permits    <- read_csv(paste0(data_path, "permits_2020.csv"),
                       show_col_types = FALSE) %>% clean_names()

# Load VIOLATION_DEFINITION.csv if available for tier mapping display
violation_def_path <- paste0(data_path, "VIOLATION_DEFINITION.csv")
if (file.exists(violation_def_path)) {
  violation_def <- read_csv(violation_def_path, show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    clean_names()
} else {
  violation_def <- NULL
  message("VIOLATION_DEFINITION.csv not found; skipping code title display.")
}

cat("properties:", comma(nrow(properties)), "\n")
cat("clean_seal:", comma(nrow(clean_seal)), "\n")
cat("violations:", comma(nrow(violations)), "\n")
cat("licenses:  ", comma(nrow(licenses)),   "\n")
cat("unsafe:    ", comma(nrow(unsafe)),     "\n")
cat("permits:   ", comma(nrow(permits)),    "\n")
cat("imm_dang:  ", comma(nrow(imm_dang)),   "\n")
cat("parcel_sf: ", comma(nrow(parcel_sf)),  "\n")
```

---

## 2. Construct OVS (Observed Vacancy Status)

Following the VacancyGuide2026 methodology, OVS=1 if any of the following are met:

- **Source 1: Clean & Seal** — Property appears in L&I Clean & Seal records
- **Source 2: Vacant Violations** — Has vacancy-related violation codes with status = 'OPEN'.
- **Source 3: Vacant License** — Holds an active vacant property license (Residential 3219 or Commercial 3634).

### 2a. Source 1 — Clean & Seal

```{r ovs-cleanseal}
# Identify parcels with completed demolition permits following a Clean & Seal event
demo_after_seal <- clean_seal %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  select(opa_account_num, casecreateddate) %>%
  inner_join(
    permits %>%
      filter(tolower(permittype) == "demolition",
             !is.na(permitcompleteddate),
             tolower(status) == "completed") %>%
      select(opa_account_num, permitcompleteddate),
    by = "opa_account_num"
  ) %>%
  filter(permitcompleteddate > casecreateddate) %>%
  distinct(opa_account_num)

cs_parcels <- clean_seal %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  # Fichman fix 1: within a 2 year window
  filter(casecreateddate >= (TRAIN_CUTOFF - lubridate::years(2))) %>%
  # Fichman fix 2: demolition after clean and seal = empty lot
  anti_join(demo_after_seal, by = "opa_account_num") %>%
  distinct(opa_account_num) %>%
  mutate(ovs_clean_seal = 1)

cat("Unique Clean & Seal parcels (2yr window + demolition filter):", comma(nrow(cs_parcels)), "\n")
```

### 2b. Source 2 — Open Vacant Violations

```{r ovs-violations}
# 11 vacancy violation codes used for OVS definition (not used as predictors)
ovs_viol_codes <- c(
  "9-3904", "9-3905", "PM15-901.1", "PM15-901.2", "PM15-301",
  "CP-102",  "CP-103", "PM-102.4/1", "PM-102.4/2",
  "PM-102.4/3", "PM-102.4/4"
)

viol_parcels <- violations %>%
  filter(violationcode %in% ovs_viol_codes,
         tolower(violationstatus) == "open") %>%
  distinct(opa_account_num) %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(ovs_violation = 1)

cat("Parcels with OPEN vacant violations:", comma(nrow(viol_parcels)), "\n")

violations %>%
  filter(violationcode %in% ovs_viol_codes,
         tolower(violationstatus) == "open") %>%
  count(violationcode, sort = TRUE) %>%
  kable(caption = "Distribution of OPEN Vacant Violation Codes") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### 2c. Source 3 — Active Vacant License

```{r ovs-license}
# 3219 = Residential Vacant, 3634 = Commercial Vacant
vacant_license_parcels <- licenses %>%
  filter(tolower(licensestatus) == "active",
         revenuecode %in% c("3219", "3634")) %>%
  distinct(opa_account_num) %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(ovs_license = 1)

cat("Parcels with active vacant licenses:", comma(nrow(vacant_license_parcels)), "\n")
```

### 2d. Merge OVS to Properties

```{r ovs-merge}
ovs_df <- properties %>%
  select(parcel_number, location, owner_1, category_code, category_code_description,
         year_built, total_livable_area, market_value, sale_date, sale_price,
         exterior_condition, interior_condition,
         zoning, zip_code, census_tract) %>%
  left_join(cs_parcels,             by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  left_join(viol_parcels,           by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  left_join(vacant_license_parcels, by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  replace_na(list(ovs_clean_seal = 0, ovs_violation = 0, ovs_license = 0)) %>%
  mutate(
    ovs         = if_else(ovs_clean_seal == 1 | ovs_violation == 1 | ovs_license == 1, 1, 0),
    ovs_sources = ovs_clean_seal + ovs_violation + ovs_license
  )

cat("Total parcels:", comma(nrow(ovs_df)), "\n")
cat("OVS=1:  ", comma(sum(ovs_df$ovs == 1)),
    "(", percent(mean(ovs_df$ovs), 0.1), ")\n")
cat("OVS=0:  ", comma(sum(ovs_df$ovs == 0)), "\n")
```

### 2e. Filter for Residential + Join Geometry

Includes: Single Family (1), Multi-Family (2), Mixed (3), Large Apartments > 4 units (14).

```{r filter-attach}
ovs_df <- ovs_df %>%
  filter(category_code %in% c("1", "2", "3", "14")) %>%
  left_join(
    parcel_sf %>% select(brt_id, bldg_desc, shape_area, shape_length, geometry),
    by = c("parcel_number" = "brt_id")
  ) %>%
  st_as_sf()

cat("Post-filter parcels:", comma(nrow(ovs_df)), "\n")
cat("OVS=1:", comma(sum(ovs_df$ovs == 1)),
    "(", percent(mean(ovs_df$ovs), 0.1), ")\n")
```

### 2f. OVS Source Overlap Analysis

```{r ovs-overlap}
ovs_positive <- ovs_df %>% filter(ovs == 1)

cat("=== OVS=1 Source Breakdown ===\n")
cat("Clean & Seal Only:", comma(sum(ovs_positive$ovs_clean_seal==1 & ovs_positive$ovs_violation==0 & ovs_positive$ovs_license==0)), "\n")
cat("Violation Only:   ", comma(sum(ovs_positive$ovs_clean_seal==0 & ovs_positive$ovs_violation==1 & ovs_positive$ovs_license==0)), "\n")
cat("License Only:     ", comma(sum(ovs_positive$ovs_clean_seal==0 & ovs_positive$ovs_violation==0 & ovs_positive$ovs_license==1)), "\n")
cat("Two-source overlap:       ", comma(sum(ovs_positive$ovs_sources == 2)), "\n")
cat("Three-source overlap:       ", comma(sum(ovs_positive$ovs_sources == 3)), "\n")
```

```{r ovs-overlap-plot, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
p_combo <- ovs_positive %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(source_combo = case_when(
    ovs_clean_seal==1 & ovs_violation==1 & ovs_license==1 ~ "All Three",
    ovs_clean_seal==1 & ovs_violation==1 ~ "Clean&Seal + Violation",
    ovs_clean_seal==1 & ovs_license==1   ~ "Clean&Seal + License",
    ovs_violation==1  & ovs_license==1   ~ "Violation + License",
    ovs_clean_seal==1 ~ "Clean & Seal Only",
    ovs_violation==1  ~ "Violation Only",
    ovs_license==1    ~ "License Only"
  )) %>%
  count(source_combo, sort = TRUE) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(source_combo, n), y = n)) +
  geom_col(fill = "steelblue") +
  geom_text(aes(label = comma(n)), hjust = -0.1, size = 3.5) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))) +
  labs(title = "Distribution of OVS=1 Parcels by Source Combination", x = NULL, y = "parcel count") +
  theme_minimal()

p_combo
ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "ovs_combination_plot.png"),
       plot = p_combo, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

---

## 3. VacancyGuide Violation Tier Mapping

Constructs the violation code tier table (108 codes) for feature engineering.

VacancyGuide2026 has three tiers (+1/+2/+3), totalling 108 codes.

**Note (Fichman feedback, 2026-02-18):**
Tier weights were established via trial-and-error without statistical validation (Tim Haynes at 1/27 kickoff meeting).

We use these tiers for for Track B comparison rather than primary predictors.

### 3a. Define VacancyGuide Code Lists

```{r vacancyguide-codes}
# Level 1: +1 Point — Minor deterioration and general maintenance
guide_level1 <- c(
  "PM15-304.10", "PM15-304.11", "PM15-304.19", "PM15-304.19O",
  "PM15-304.2",  "PM15-304.8",  "PM15-308.1",  "PM15-309.1",
  "PM15-309.3",  "PM15-309.4",  "PM-302.2/1",  "PM-302.2/2",
  "PM-302.3/1",  "PM-302.4/6",  "PM-302.4/7",  "PM-304.1/1",
  "PM-304.6/1",  "PM-304.8/1",  "PM-304.8/15", "PM-304.8/16",
  "PM-304.8/17", "PM-304.8/18", "PM-304.8/19", "PM-304.8/2",
  "PM-304.8/20", "PM-304.8/21"
)

# Level 2: +2 Points — Moderate structural damage
guide_level2 <- c(
  "PM15-110.7",  "PM15-302.1",  "PM15-302.4",  "PM15-302.5",
  "PM15-304.13", "PM15-304.15", "PM15-304.16", "PM15-304.4",
  "PM15-304.5",  "PM15-304.6",  "PM15-304.7",  "PM-304.2/1",
  "PM-304.3/1",  "PM-304.3/2",  "PM-304.3/3",  "PM-304.3/4",
  "PM-304.3/5",  "PM-304.5/2",  "PM-304.5/3"
)

# Level 3: +3 Points — Severe: Vacant/Unsafe/Collapse
guide_level3 <- c(
  "9-3905",       "CP-103",       "PM-102.4/2",   "PM-102.4/4",
  "PM15-108.1",   "PM15-108.2",   "PM15-109.1",   "PM15-110.1",
  "PM15-301",     "PM15-304.1",   "PM15-304.19V", "PM15-304.1A",
  "PM15-304.1B",  "PM15-304.1C",  "PM15-304.1D",  "PM15-304.1E",
  "PM15-304.1F",  "PM15-304.1G",  "PM15-304.1H",  "PM15-304.1I",
  "PM15-304.1J",  "PM15-901.1",   "PM15-901.2",   "PM15-902.1",
  "PM-304.4/1",   "PM-306.0/1",   "PM-306.0/90",  "PM-306.2/1",
  "PM-306.2/4",   "PM-306.2/90",  "PM-306.2/91",  "PM-306.2/92",
  "PM-307.1/1",   "PM-307.1/11",  "PM-307.1/12",  "PM-307.1/13",
  "PM-307.1/19",  "PM-307.1/2",   "PM-307.1/3",   "PM-307.1/4",
  "PM-307.1/5",   "PM-307.1/6",   "PM-307.1/7",   "PM-307.1/8",
  "PM-307.1/9",   "PM-307.1/90",  "PM-307.2/1",   "PM-307.3/1",
  "PM-307.3/2",   "PM-307.3/90",  "PM-307.3/91",  "PM-308.1/1",
  "PM-308.1/10",  "PM-308.1/11",  "PM-308.1/12",  "PM-308.1/2",
  "PM-308.1/3",   "PM-308.1/4",   "PM-308.1/5",   "PM-308.1/6",
  "PM-308.1/7",   "PM-308.1/8",   "PM-308.1/9"
)

# Vacant Land Codes (For reference only; building model does not use these)
guide_land <- c(
  "CP-102", "PM-102.4/1", "PM-102.4/3", "PM-302.2/4",
  "PM-304.9/2", "PM-304.9/3", "PM-304.9/4", "PM-306.0/2", "PM-306.0/91"
)

building_all <- c(guide_level1, guide_level2, guide_level3)

cat("Level 1 (+1 pt):", length(guide_level1), "codes\n")
cat("Level 2 (+2 pts):", length(guide_level2), "codes\n")
cat("Level 3 (+3 pts):", length(guide_level3), "codes\n")
cat("Building Model Total: ", length(building_all), "codes\n")
cat("Land Model:     ", length(guide_land),   "codes\n")

# Duplicate verification
stopifnot("Duplicate codes found across building tiers!" =
          length(building_all) == length(unique(building_all)))
cat("No duplicate codes between building tiers\n")
```

### 3b. Construct Full Tier Mapping Table

```{r complete-mapping}
building_tier_map <- bind_rows(
  tibble(code = guide_level1, guide_tier = "Level 1", guide_weight = 1L),
  tibble(code = guide_level2, guide_tier = "Level 2", guide_weight = 2L),
  tibble(code = guide_level3, guide_tier = "Level 3", guide_weight = 3L)
) %>%
  mutate(in_ovs_def = code %in% ovs_viol_codes)

# Append titles if violation_def exists
if (!is.null(violation_def)) {
  building_tier_map <- building_tier_map %>%
    left_join(violation_def %>% select(violation_code, violation_code_title),
              by = c("code" = "violation_code"))
}

building_tier_map %>%
  count(guide_tier, guide_weight) %>%
  kable(caption = "VacancyGuide Building Violation Tier Summary") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
```

### 3c. Validation: Tier Code Frequency in Real Data

```{r validate-tiers}
building_viol_counts <- violations %>%
  filter(violationcode %in% building_all,
         tolower(violationstatus) == "open") %>%
  count(violationcode, name = "n_open") %>%
  right_join(building_tier_map %>% select(code, guide_tier, guide_weight),
             by = c("violationcode" = "code")) %>%
  replace_na(list(n_open = 0)) %>%
  arrange(guide_weight, desc(n_open))

building_viol_counts %>%
  group_by(guide_tier) %>%
  summarise(
    codes_with_data = sum(n_open > 0),
    total_codes     = n(),
    total_open      = sum(n_open),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Tier","Codes w/ Data", "Total Codes", "Total OPEN Violations"),
        caption = "OPEN Violation Counts per Tier in Current Data") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
```

### 3d. 54 Vacancy Keyword Codes (Track A)

```{r vacancy-keyword-codes}
# Screen for codes where title or definition contains "vacant"
if (!is.null(violation_def)) {
  vacancy_keyword_codes <- violation_def %>%
    filter(str_detect(tolower(violation_code_title), "vacant") |
           str_detect(tolower(violation_definition),  "vacant")) %>%
    pull(violation_code)
  cat("Vacancy keyword codes found:", length(vacancy_keyword_codes), "\n")
} else {
  # fallback: use known vacancy codes from Level 3
  vacancy_keyword_codes <- c(
    ovs_viol_codes,
    "PM15-108.1", "PM15-108.2", "PM15-109.1", "PM15-110.1",
    "PM15-304.1",  "PM15-304.19V", "PM15-901.1", "PM15-901.2",
    "PM15-902.1",  "PM-306.0/1",   "PM-306.2/1", "PM-307.1/1",
    "PM-307.2/1",  "PM-307.3/1"
  ) %>% unique()
  cat("Using fallback vacancy codes, count:", length(vacancy_keyword_codes), "\n")
}

# Maintenance codes (Level 1+2 excluding vacancy keywords)
maintenance_codes <- building_tier_map %>%
  filter(guide_weight %in% c(1, 2)) %>%
  pull(code)

# Structural codes (Level 3)
structural_codes <- building_tier_map %>%
  filter(guide_weight == 3) %>%
  pull(code)
```

---

## 4. EDA — Key Variable Exploration

### 4a. Exterior Condition & Vacancy Rate

> Exterior condition is an ordered categorical variable (1–7); we use a bar
> chart to show the vacancy rate at each level.
> (Fichman feedback: violin plots are not appropriate for ordered categorical variables.)

```{r eda-exterior, fig.width=7, fig.height=4}
p_ext <- ovs_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(exterior_condition)) %>%
  mutate(exterior_condition = factor(as.integer(exterior_condition))) %>%
  group_by(exterior_condition) %>%
  summarise(n = n(), n_vacant = sum(ovs == 1),
            vac_rate = mean(ovs == 1), .groups = "drop") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = exterior_condition, y = vac_rate, fill = exterior_condition)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE) +
  geom_text(aes(label = percent(vac_rate, 0.1)), vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "OrRd") +
  labs(title    = "Vacancy Rate by Exterior Condition Rating",
       subtitle = "1 = New/Excellent → 7 = Collapsed/Deteriorated",
       x = "Exterior Condition (1–7)", y = "OVS=1 Rate") +
  theme_minimal()

p_ext
ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "exterior_condition_vacancy_rate.png"),
       plot = p_ext, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

### 4b. OVS=1 Analysis in New Construction

> Fichman feedback: why are new-construction (post-2010) properties showing up as OVS=1?

```{r eda-new-vacant}
new_vacant <- ovs_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(!is.na(year_built), as.integer(year_built) >= 2010, ovs == 1)

cat("Parcels built after 2010 with OVS=1:", nrow(new_vacant), "\n\n")

bind_rows(
  new_vacant %>%
    summarise(group = "New Construction (2010+) & Vacant",
              pct_clean_seal = mean(ovs_clean_seal == 1),
              pct_violation  = mean(ovs_violation  == 1),
              pct_license    = mean(ovs_license    == 1)),
  ovs_df %>% st_drop_geometry() %>% filter(ovs == 1) %>%
    summarise(group = "All OVS=1 (Baseline)",
              pct_clean_seal = mean(ovs_clean_seal == 1),
              pct_violation  = mean(ovs_violation  == 1),
              pct_license    = mean(ovs_license    == 1))
) %>%
  mutate(across(where(is.numeric), ~percent(.x, 0.1))) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Group", "Clean & Seal %", "Violation %", "License %"),
        caption = "New Vacant vs. All Vacant: OVS Source Composition") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = "striped", full_width = FALSE)
```

### 4c. Violation History vs. Vacancy (Fichman: faceted plot)

> Fichman feedback: plot the mean violation count over different historical
> time windows, grouped by OVS=1/0. This is the core EDA for validating that
> life-history features are meaningful.

```{r eda-violations-faceted, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
# Violation counts in different windows (all codes included for frequency analysis)
viol_for_eda <- violations %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(
    violationdate = as.Date(violationdate),
    is_pre        = !is.na(violationdate) & violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_5yr        = !is.na(violationdate) &
                      violationdate >= (TRAIN_CUTOFF - lubridate::years(5)) &
                      violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_3yr        = !is.na(violationdate) &
                      violationdate >= (TRAIN_CUTOFF - lubridate::years(3)) &
                      violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_recent     = !is.na(violationdate) &
                      violationdate >= RECENT_WINDOW & violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF
  ) %>%
  filter(is_pre) %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(
    n_viol_total  = n(),
    n_viol_5yr    = sum(is_5yr),
    n_viol_3yr    = sum(is_3yr),
    n_viol_recent = sum(is_recent),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

viol_eda_df <- ovs_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  select(parcel_number, ovs) %>%
  left_join(viol_for_eda, by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  mutate(
    across(starts_with("n_viol_"), ~replace_na(.x, 0)),
    ovs_label = if_else(ovs == 1, "OVS=1 (Vacant)", "OVS=0 (Occupied)")
  )

# Compute group means and pivot to long format
viol_means <- viol_eda_df %>%
  group_by(ovs_label) %>%
  summarise(
    `All Time`      = mean(n_viol_total),
    `Last 5 Years`  = mean(n_viol_5yr),
    `Last 3 Years`  = mean(n_viol_3yr),
    `Last 6 Months` = mean(n_viol_recent),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  pivot_longer(-ovs_label, names_to = "window", values_to = "mean_violations") %>%
  mutate(window = factor(window,
                         levels = c("All Time", "Last 5 Years",
                                    "Last 3 Years", "Last 6 Months")))

p_viol_facet <- viol_means %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = ovs_label, y = mean_violations, fill = ovs_label)) +
  geom_col(show.legend = FALSE, width = 0.6) +
  geom_text(aes(label = round(mean_violations, 2)),
            vjust = -0.4, size = 3.5) +
  facet_wrap(~window, scales = "free_y") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("OVS=0 (Occupied)" = "steelblue",
                                "OVS=1 (Vacant)"  = "tomato")) +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.2))) +
  labs(title    = "Mean Number of Violations by Vacancy Status",
       subtitle = "Across different time windows | properties with no violations counted as 0",
       x = NULL, y = "Mean Violations per Parcel") +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"))

p_viol_facet
ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "violations_faceted_by_ovs.png"),
       plot = p_viol_facet, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
```

---

## 5. Baseline Model (OPA Fields Only)

The baseline uses only the OPA fields already on `ovs_df` — no extra joins.
This is the floor for model performance; the engineered model in Section 7
must beat this AUC.

| Included | Rationale |
|----------|-----------|
| `exterior_condition` | Physical state directly observed by an assessor |
| `log(market_value)` | Economic disinvestment signal; log normalizes right skew |
| `building_age` | Derived from `year_built` |
| `log(total_livable_area)` | Proxy for building floor area |

### 5a. Prepare Dataset

```{r baseline-prep}
baseline_df <- ovs_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  filter(market_value > 0, total_livable_area > 0, !is.na(exterior_condition)) %>%
  mutate(
    exterior_condition = as.numeric(exterior_condition),
    building_age       = 2024L - as.integer(year_built),
    building_age       = if_else(building_age < 0 | building_age > 200,
                                 NA_integer_, building_age),
    log_market_value   = log(market_value),
    log_livable_area   = log(total_livable_area)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(building_age))

cat("Baseline dataset:", comma(nrow(baseline_df)), "parcels\n")
cat("OVS=1:", comma(sum(baseline_df$ovs == 1)),
    "(", percent(mean(baseline_df$ovs), 0.1), ")\n")
```

### 5b. Fit model

```{r baseline-model}
baseline_model <- glm(
  ovs ~ exterior_condition + log_market_value + building_age + log_livable_area,
  data = baseline_df, family = binomial()
)
summary(baseline_model)
```

### 5c. Fit Statistics & Odds Ratios

```{r baseline-fit}
null_dev    <- baseline_model$null.deviance
resid_dev   <- baseline_model$deviance

tibble(
  Metric = c("McFadden R²", "AUC (training set)", "AIC", "N"),
  Value  = c(round(1 - resid_dev/null_dev, 3),
             "— (see ROC below)",
             round(AIC(baseline_model), 1),
             comma(nobs(baseline_model)))
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Baseline fit statistics") %>%
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)

tidy(baseline_model, exponentiate = TRUE, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  select(term, estimate, conf.low, conf.high, p.value) %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), ~round(.x, 3)),
         p.value = format.pval(p.value, digits = 3)) %>%
  kable(caption = "Baseline Odds Ratios") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

### 5d. Threshold & Probability Distribution

> Fichman feedback: use a predicted-probability density plot instead of discussing a single threshold number.

```{r baseline-threshold}
pred_probs_baseline <- predict(baseline_model, type = "response")
actual_baseline     <- baseline_df$ovs

roc_baseline  <- roc(actual_baseline, pred_probs_baseline, quiet = TRUE)
best_baseline <- coords(roc_baseline, "best", best.method = "youden",
                        ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

cat("Baseline AUC:       ", round(auc(roc_baseline), 3), "\n")
cat("Youden optimal threshold:", round(best_baseline$threshold, 3), "\n")
cat("Sensitivity:        ", round(best_baseline$sensitivity, 3), "\n")
cat("Specificity:        ", round(best_baseline$specificity, 3), "\n")
```

```{r baseline-prob-dist, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
p_prob_baseline <- tibble(
  prob  = pred_probs_baseline,
  label = factor(actual_baseline, levels = c(0,1),
                 labels = c("OVS=0 (Occupied)", "OVS=1 (Vacant)"))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = prob, fill = label)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = best_baseline$threshold,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = best_baseline$threshold + 0.005, y = Inf,
           label = paste0("Youden = ", round(best_baseline$threshold, 3)),
           vjust = 2, hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue", "tomato")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(title    = "Baseline: Predicted Probability Distribution by True OVS",
       subtitle = paste0("AUC = ", round(auc(roc_baseline), 3),
                         " | high overlap = poor separation; clear separation = good"),
       x = "Predicted P(Vacant)", y = "Density", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_prob_baseline
ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "baseline_prob_distribution.png"),
       plot = p_prob_baseline, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

```{r baseline-roc, fig.width=6, fig.height=5}
ggroc(roc_baseline, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_abline(slope = 1, intercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "gray50") +
  annotate("point", x = best_baseline$specificity,
           y = best_baseline$sensitivity, color = "tomato", size = 4) +
  labs(title    = "ROC Curve — Baseline Model",
       subtitle = paste0("AUC = ", round(auc(roc_baseline), 3)),
       x = "Specificity", y = "Sensitivity") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r baseline-cm}
pred_class_baseline <- if_else(pred_probs_baseline >= best_baseline$threshold, 1L, 0L)
cm_b <- table(Predicted = pred_class_baseline, Actual = actual_baseline)
print(cm_b)
cat("Sensitivity:", round(cm_b[2,2]/(cm_b[1,2]+cm_b[2,2]), 3), "\n")
cat("Specificity:", round(cm_b[1,1]/(cm_b[1,1]+cm_b[2,1]), 3), "\n")
cat("Precision:  ", round(cm_b[2,2]/(cm_b[2,1]+cm_b[2,2]), 3), "\n")
```

> **Baseline:** the engineered model must beat AUC = `r round(auc(roc_baseline), 3)`.

---

## 6. Feature Engineering

### Design principles

VacancyGuide tier weights were set by trial-and-error with no statistical
validation:
- **Track A (primary):** use raw violation features and let the model learn
  which patterns actually predict OVS.
- **Track B (comparison):** keep the tier features; the univariate analysis
  in Section 9 decides what to keep or drop.

All features are filtered with `< TRAIN_CUTOFF` to strictly avoid temporal leakage.

### 6a. Violation Features (Track A)

```{r feat-violations}
viol_clean <- violations %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(
    violationdate  = as.Date(violationdate),
    is_open        = as.integer(tolower(violationstatus) == "open"),
    is_ovs_code    = violationcode %in% ovs_viol_codes,
    is_pre         = !is.na(violationdate) & violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_recent      = !is.na(violationdate) &
                       violationdate >= RECENT_WINDOW & violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_3yr         = !is.na(violationdate) &
                       violationdate >= (TRAIN_CUTOFF - lubridate::years(3)) &
                       violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_5yr         = !is.na(violationdate) &
                       violationdate >= (TRAIN_CUTOFF - lubridate::years(5)) &
                       violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF,
    is_vacancy_kw  = violationcode %in% vacancy_keyword_codes,
    is_maintenance = violationcode %in% maintenance_codes,
    is_structural  = violationcode %in% structural_codes,
    is_fire_safety = str_detect(tolower(violationcodetitle),
                                "fire|smoke|sprinkler|egress|emergency|hazard")
  ) %>%
  filter(is_pre, !is_ovs_code)

viol_features <- viol_clean %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(
    n_violations_total    = n(),
    n_violations_recent   = sum(is_recent),
    n_violations_3yr      = sum(is_3yr),       # Fichman: life history — last 3 years
    n_violations_5yr      = sum(is_5yr),       # Fichman: life history — last 5 years
    n_violations_open     = sum(is_open),
    n_distinct_codes      = n_distinct(violationcode),
    has_open_violation    = as.integer(any(is_open == 1L)),
    days_since_last_viol  = as.integer(TRAIN_CUTOFF - max(violationdate, na.rm = TRUE)),
    days_oldest_open_viol = as.integer(
      TRAIN_CUTOFF - min(violationdate[is_open == 1L], na.rm = TRUE)),
    has_vacancy_kw_code   = as.integer(any(is_vacancy_kw)),
    has_maintenance_code  = as.integer(any(is_maintenance)),
    has_structural_code   = as.integer(any(is_structural)),
    has_fire_safety_code  = as.integer(any(is_fire_safety)),
    has_open_vacancy_kw   = as.integer(any(is_open == 1L & is_vacancy_kw)),
    has_open_structural   = as.integer(any(is_open == 1L & is_structural)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(days_oldest_open_viol = if_else(
    is.infinite(days_oldest_open_viol), NA_integer_, days_oldest_open_viol))

cat("Violation features:", comma(nrow(viol_features)), "parcels\n")
```

### 6b. License Features

```{r feat-licenses}
lic_clean <- licenses %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(
    initialissuedate = as.Date(initialissuedate),
    is_active        = tolower(licensestatus) == "active",
    is_vacancy_lic   = revenuecode %in% c("3219", "3634"),
    is_rental_lic    = revenuecode == "3202",
    is_pre           = !is.na(initialissuedate) & initialissuedate < TRAIN_CUTOFF
  ) %>%
  filter(is_pre)

license_features <- lic_clean %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(
    had_vacancy_license = as.integer(any(is_vacancy_lic)),
    n_vacancy_licenses  = sum(is_vacancy_lic),
    has_rental_license  = as.integer(any(is_active & is_rental_lic)),
    n_licenses_total    = n(),
    license_lapse_rate  = sum(!is_active) / n(),
    days_since_last_lic = as.integer(TRAIN_CUTOFF - max(initialissuedate, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("License features:", comma(nrow(license_features)), "parcels\n")
```

### 6c. Unsafe & Imminently Dangerous Features

```{r feat-unsafe}
unsafe_features <- unsafe %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(violationdate = as.Date(violationdate)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(violationdate), violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF) %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(has_unsafe = 1L, n_unsafe = n(),
            days_since_unsafe = as.integer(TRAIN_CUTOFF - max(violationdate, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

imd_features <- imm_dang %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num)) %>%
  mutate(violationdate = as.Date(violationdate)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(violationdate), violationdate < TRAIN_CUTOFF) %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(has_imd = 1L, n_imd = n(),
            days_since_imd = as.integer(TRAIN_CUTOFF - max(violationdate, na.rm = TRUE)),
            .groups = "drop")

cat("Unsafe:", comma(nrow(unsafe_features)), "| IMD:", comma(nrow(imd_features)), "\n")
```

### 6d. OPA-derived Features

```{r feat-opa}
opa_features <- ovs_df %>%
  st_drop_geometry() %>%
  mutate(
    exterior_condition = as.numeric(exterior_condition),
    building_age       = 2024L - as.integer(year_built),
    building_age       = if_else(building_age < 0 | building_age > 200,
                                 NA_integer_, building_age),
    log_market_value   = log1p(market_value),
    log_livable_area   = log1p(total_livable_area),
    log_sale_price     = log1p(sale_price),
    days_since_sale    = as.integer(TRAIN_CUTOFF - as.Date(sale_date)),
    days_since_sale    = if_else(days_since_sale < 0, NA_integer_, days_since_sale),
    years_since_sale   = days_since_sale / 365.25,  # Fichman: life history — years since last sale
    is_poor_condition  = as.integer(exterior_condition >= 5),
    value_per_sqft     = if_else(total_livable_area > 0,
                                 market_value / total_livable_area, NA_real_)
  ) %>%
  select(parcel_number, exterior_condition, building_age,
         log_market_value, log_livable_area, log_sale_price,
         days_since_sale, years_since_sale, is_poor_condition, value_per_sqft,
         zoning, zip_code, census_tract,
         ovs, ovs_clean_seal, ovs_violation, ovs_license)
```

---

## 7. Assemble the full feature dataset

```{r assemble}
features_df <- opa_features %>%
  left_join(viol_features,    by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  left_join(license_features, by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  left_join(unsafe_features,  by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  left_join(imd_features,     by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  mutate(
    across(c(n_violations_total, n_violations_recent, n_violations_3yr, n_violations_5yr,
             n_violations_open, n_distinct_codes, has_open_violation,
             has_vacancy_kw_code, has_maintenance_code, has_structural_code,
             has_fire_safety_code, has_open_vacancy_kw, has_open_structural,
             n_licenses_total, had_vacancy_license, n_vacancy_licenses,
             has_rental_license, has_unsafe, n_unsafe, has_imd, n_imd),
           ~replace_na(.x, 0L)),
    license_lapse_rate = replace_na(license_lapse_rate, 0)
  )

cat("Feature dataset:", comma(nrow(features_df)), "rows x", ncol(features_df), "columns\n")
cat("OVS=1:", comma(sum(features_df$ovs==1, na.rm=TRUE)),
    "(", percent(mean(features_df$ovs, na.rm=TRUE), 0.1), ")\n")
```

---

## 8. Missing-value audit

```{r missing-audit}
features_df %>%
  select(-parcel_number, -zip_code, -census_tract, -zoning, -starts_with("ovs_")) %>%
  summarise(across(everything(), ~sum(is.na(.)))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "feature", values_to = "n_missing") %>%
  mutate(
    pct_missing = n_missing / nrow(features_df),
    flag = case_when(pct_missing == 0   ~ "Complete",
                     pct_missing < 0.05 ~ "< 5%",
                     pct_missing < 0.20 ~ "5–20%",
                     TRUE               ~ "> 20%")
  ) %>%
  filter(pct_missing > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(pct_missing)) %>%
  mutate(pct_missing = percent(pct_missing, 0.1)) %>%
  kable(col.names = c("Feature", "N Missing", "% Missing", "Flag"),
        caption = "Missing-value audit") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

---

## 9. Univariate correlation screen

```{r univariate-corr, fig.width=10, fig.height=6}
corr_df <- features_df %>%
  select(ovs, where(is.numeric), -starts_with("ovs_")) %>%
  summarise(across(-ovs, ~cor(.x, ovs, use = "complete.obs"))) %>%
  pivot_longer(everything(), names_to = "feature", values_to = "r") %>%
  mutate(abs_r     = abs(r),
         direction = if_else(r > 0, "Positive (→ vacant)", "Negative (→ occupied)")) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_r)) %>%
  head(20)

p_corr <- corr_df %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(feature, abs_r), y = r, fill = direction)) +
  geom_col() +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Positive (→ vacant)"   = "tomato",
                                "Negative (→ occupied)" = "steelblue")) +
  labs(title = "Top 20 Features: Correlation with OVS", x = NULL, y = "Pearson r",
       fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_corr
ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "univariate_correlation.png"),
       plot = p_corr, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

corr_df %>% mutate(r = round(r, 3)) %>% select(feature, r, direction) %>%
  kable(caption = "Top 20 feature-vs-OVS correlations") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

---

## 10. Engineered Model: random vs temporal split

### 10a. Modeling data frame

```{r model-df}
model_df <- features_df %>%
  filter(!is.na(exterior_condition), !is.na(building_age),
         !is.na(log_market_value),   !is.na(value_per_sqft)) %>%
  mutate(
    days_since_last_viol  = replace_na(days_since_last_viol,
                              as.integer(TRAIN_CUTOFF - as.Date("2000-01-01"))),
    days_oldest_open_viol = replace_na(days_oldest_open_viol, 0L)
  )

cat("Modeling dataset:", comma(nrow(model_df)), "parcels |",
    "OVS=1:", percent(mean(model_df$ovs), 0.1), "\n")
```

### 10b. Temporal split assignment

```{r temporal-assign}
latest_viol <- violations %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num), !is.na(violationdate)) %>%
  mutate(violationdate = as.Date(violationdate)) %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(latest_viol = max(violationdate, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

latest_lic <- licenses %>%
  filter(!is.na(opa_account_num), !is.na(initialissuedate)) %>%
  mutate(initialissuedate = as.Date(initialissuedate)) %>%
  group_by(opa_account_num) %>%
  summarise(latest_lic = max(initialissuedate, na.rm = TRUE), .groups = "drop")

model_df <- model_df %>%
  left_join(latest_viol, by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  left_join(latest_lic,  by = c("parcel_number" = "opa_account_num")) %>%
  mutate(
    latest_activity = pmax(latest_viol, latest_lic, na.rm = TRUE),
    temporal_split  = case_when(
      is.na(latest_activity)                                    ~ "train",
      as.numeric(latest_activity) <= quantile(as.numeric(latest_activity), 0.70, na.rm=TRUE) ~ "train",
      TRUE                                                       ~ "test"
    )
  )

model_df %>% count(temporal_split) %>% mutate(pct = percent(n/sum(n), 0.1)) %>%
  kable() %>% kable_styling(full_width = FALSE)
```

### 10c. Model formula

```{r formula}
model_formula <- ovs ~
  n_violations_total + n_violations_recent + n_violations_open + n_distinct_codes +
  days_since_last_viol + days_oldest_open_viol +
  has_vacancy_kw_code + has_maintenance_code + has_structural_code +
  has_fire_safety_code + has_open_structural +
  had_vacancy_license + has_rental_license + license_lapse_rate +
  exterior_condition + building_age + has_unsafe + has_imd +
  log_market_value + value_per_sqft
```

### 10d. Split A — random 70/30

```{r random-split}
set.seed(42)
idx          <- sample(nrow(model_df), floor(0.7 * nrow(model_df)))
train_random <- model_df[idx, ]
test_random  <- model_df[-idx, ]

model_random  <- glm(model_formula, data = train_random, family = binomial())
prob_random   <- predict(model_random, newdata = test_random, type = "response")
roc_random    <- roc(test_random$ovs, prob_random, quiet = TRUE)
thresh_random <- coords(roc_random, "best", best.method = "youden",
                        ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

cat("Random split — AUC:", round(auc(roc_random), 3),
    "| Sens:", round(thresh_random$sensitivity, 3),
    "| Spec:", round(thresh_random$specificity, 3), "\n")
```

### 10e. Split B — temporal 70/30

```{r temporal-model}
train_temporal <- model_df %>% filter(temporal_split == "train")
test_temporal  <- model_df %>% filter(temporal_split == "test")

model_temporal  <- glm(model_formula, data = train_temporal, family = binomial())
prob_temporal   <- predict(model_temporal, newdata = test_temporal, type = "response")
roc_temporal    <- roc(test_temporal$ovs, prob_temporal, quiet = TRUE)
thresh_temporal <- coords(roc_temporal, "best", best.method = "youden",
                          ret = c("threshold", "sensitivity", "specificity"))

cat("Temporal split — AUC:", round(auc(roc_temporal), 3),
    "| Sens:", round(thresh_temporal$sensitivity, 3),
    "| Spec:", round(thresh_temporal$specificity, 3), "\n")
```

### 10f. Comparison table + probability distribution plot

```{r split-comparison}
tibble(
  Split       = c("Random 70/30", "Temporal 70/30"),
  AUC         = c(round(auc(roc_random), 3),    round(auc(roc_temporal), 3)),
  Sensitivity = c(round(thresh_random$sensitivity, 3),  round(thresh_temporal$sensitivity, 3)),
  Specificity = c(round(thresh_random$specificity, 3),  round(thresh_temporal$specificity, 3)),
  Threshold   = c(round(thresh_random$threshold, 3),    round(thresh_temporal$threshold, 3))
) %>%
  kable(caption = "Random vs temporal split | AUC gap < 0.03 = stable across time") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "condensed"), full_width = FALSE)
```

```{r engineered-prob-dist, fig.width=8, fig.height=5}
p_prob_eng <- tibble(
  prob  = prob_random,
  label = factor(test_random$ovs, levels=c(0,1),
                 labels=c("OVS=0 (Occupied)", "OVS=1 (Vacant)"))
) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = prob, fill = label)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = thresh_random$threshold,
             linetype = "dashed", color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  annotate("text", x = thresh_random$threshold + 0.005, y = Inf,
           label = paste0("Youden = ", round(thresh_random$threshold, 3)),
           vjust = 2, hjust = 0, size = 3.5) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("steelblue", "tomato")) +
  scale_x_continuous(labels = percent_format()) +
  labs(title    = "Engineered Model: Predicted Probability Distribution by True OVS",
       subtitle = paste0("AUC = ", round(auc(roc_random), 3),
                         " (Random) | Baseline AUC = ",
                         round(auc(roc_baseline), 3)),
       x = "Predicted P(Vacant)", y = "Density", fill = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

p_prob_eng
ggsave(paste0(graph_path, "engineered_prob_distribution.png"),
       plot = p_prob_eng, width = 9, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

```{r roc-compare, fig.width=7, fig.height=5}
ggroc(list(`Random 70/30` = roc_random, `Temporal 70/30` = roc_temporal),
      linewidth = 1) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("Random 70/30" = "steelblue",
                                 "Temporal 70/30" = "tomato")) +
  geom_abline(slope=1, intercept=1, linetype="dashed", color="gray50") +
  annotate("text", x=0.3, y=0.15,
           label = paste0("Random AUC = ",    round(auc(roc_random), 3),
                          "\nTemporal AUC = ", round(auc(roc_temporal), 3)),
           size=4, hjust=0) +
  labs(title = "ROC Curves: Random vs. Temporal Split",
       x = "Specificity", y = "Sensitivity", color = NULL) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")
```

---

## 11. Export

```{r export}
features_df %>%
  write_csv(paste0(out_path, "features_residential.csv"))

building_tier_map %>%
  write_csv(paste0(out_path, "building_tier_mapping.csv"))

tibble(code = vacancy_keyword_codes) %>%
  write_csv(paste0(out_path, "vacancy_keyword_codes.csv"))

cat("Exported:\n")
cat("  features_residential.csv  —", comma(nrow(features_df)), "rows\n")
cat("  building_tier_mapping.csv —", nrow(building_tier_map), "rows\n")
cat("  vacancy_keyword_codes.csv —", length(vacancy_keyword_codes), "rows\n")
```

---
